docs.title: Welcome to the Player and Run Tracker API
docs.text: This documentation provides a complete reference for the API, including all available endpoints, request parameters, and response schemas.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The API is built with FastAPI and provides endpoints for:

  • Player management (creation, authentication, stats)
  • Game run tracking (start, update, end)
  • Analytics (leaderboards, player summaries)
  • Admin operations

Navigate to the API Reference section to see the detailed endpoint documentation.

docs.title: Player and Run Tracker API v1.0.0
Player and Run Tracker API v1.0.0
docs.text:
Scroll down for code samples, example requests and responses. Select a language for code samples from the tabs above or the mobile navigation menu.

An API for tracking player data and game runs.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: • HTTP Authentication, scheme: basic

docs.title: Check Name
docs.text:
Code samples

# You can also use wget
curl -X POST /players/check-name \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json'


import requests
headers = {
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Accept': 'application/json'
}

r = requests.post('/players/check-name', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

POST /players/check-name

Checks if a player name is already taken. This is useful for real-time validation in the user interface.

Body parameter

{
  "player_name": "string"
}

docs.title: Parameters
Responses
docs.text: Status Meaning Description Schema 200 OK Successful Response NameCheckResponse 422 Unprocessable Entity Validation Error HTTPValidationError  This operation does not require authentication

docs.title: Generate Name
docs.text:
Code samples

# You can also use wget

  -H 'Accept: application/json'


import requests
headers = {
  'Accept': 'application/json'
}

r = requests.get('/players/generate-name', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

GET /players/generate-name

Generates a unique, random player name that is not already in use.

Example responses

200 Response

{
  "player_name": "string"
}

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: Status Meaning Description Schema 200 OK Successful Response GenerateNameResponse  This operation does not require authentication

docs.title: Start Run
docs.text:
Code samples

# You can also use wget
curl -X POST /runs/start \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json'


import requests
headers = {
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Accept': 'application/json'
}

r = requests.post('/runs/start', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

POST /runs/start

Starts a new game run. This endpoint handles both new and existing players. If create_new_player is true, a new player is created. Otherwise, the existing player is authenticated.

Body parameter

{
  "player_name": "string",
  "password": "string",
  "map_id": "string",
  "create_new_player": false
}

docs.title: Parameters
Responses
docs.text: Status Meaning Description Schema 200 OK Successful Response RunStartResponse 422 Unprocessable Entity Validation Error HTTPValidationError  This operation does not require authentication

Response Get Players Players Get

Name Type Required Restrictions Description Response Get Players Players Get [Player] false none [Schema for representing a player, including their ID and creation date.] » Player Player false none Schema for representing a player, including their ID and creation date. »» name string true none none »» id integer true none none »» created_at string(date-time) true none none This operation does not require authentication

docs.title: Create Player
docs.text:
Code samples

# You can also use wget
curl -X POST /players \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json'


import requests
headers = {
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Accept': 'application/json'
}

r = requests.post('/players', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

POST /players

Creates a new player with a randomly generated password. Returns the new player's details, including the password.

Body parameter

{
  "name": "string"
}

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
Name In Type Required Description body body PlayerCreate true none

Example responses

201 Response

{
  "name": "string",
  "id": 0,
  "created_at": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
  "password": "string"
}

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: Status Meaning Description Schema 201 Created Successful Response PlayerCreateResponse 422 Unprocessable Entity Validation Error HTTPValidationError  This operation does not require authentication

docs.title: Update Run
docs.text:
Code samples

# You can also use wget
curl -X PATCH /runs/{run_id} \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json'


import requests
headers = {
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Accept': 'application/json'
}

r = requests.patch('/runs/{run_id}', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

PATCH /runs/{run_id}

Updates the details of a specific run, such as duration, kills, and status. This is the primary endpoint for updating a run's progress.

Body parameter

{
  "duration_seconds": 0,
  "kills_total": 0,
  "level": 0,
  "xp": 0,
  "upgrades": {
    "property1": 0,
    "property2": 0
  },
  "status": "in_progress",
  "ended_at": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
  "cause_of_death": "string"
}

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
Name In Type Required Description run_id path integer true none body body RunUpdate true none

Example responses

200 Response

{
  "player_id": 0,
  "map_id": "string",
  "id": 0,
  "player": {
    "name": "string",
    "id": 0,
    "created_at": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
  },
  "started_at": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "duration_seconds": 0,
  "level": 0,
  "xp": 0,

    "property1": 0,

  },

  "cause_of_death": "string"
}

docs.title: Create Run Event
docs.text:
Code samples

# You can also use wget
curl -X POST /runs/{run_id}/events \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json'


import requests
headers = {
  'Content-Type': 'application/json',
  'Accept': 'application/json'
}

r = requests.post('/runs/{run_id}/events', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

POST /runs/{run_id}/events

Creates a new event associated with a specific run.

Body parameter

{
  "event_type": "string",
  "value": "string"
}

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text:
Name In Type Required Description run_id path integer true none body body RunEventCreate true none

Example responses

200 Response

{
  "event_type": "string",
  "value": "string",
  "id": 0,
  "run_id": 0,
  "timestamp": "2019-08-24T14:15:22Z"
}

docs.title: Responses
docs.text: Status Meaning Description Schema 200 OK Successful Response RunEvent 422 Unprocessable Entity Validation Error HTTPValidationError  This operation does not require authentication

GET /analytics/players-summary

Provides a summary of all players, including their total number of runs and best run time. Can be filtered by a search term.

docs.text:

  -H 'Accept: application/json'

}

GET /analytics/view_player_stats/{player_id}

Retrieves detailed statistics for a single player, such as total runs, average survival time, and total kills.

  "favourite_upgrade": "string",
  "total_monsters_slain": 0
}

docs.title: Test Endpoint Two
docs.text:
Code samples

# You can also use wget
curl -X GET /test2 \
  -H 'Accept: application/json'


import requests
headers = {
  'Accept': 'application/json'
}

r = requests.get('/test2', headers = headers)

print(r.json())

GET /test2

Example responses

200 Response

null
